message: The wage comparison table on page 3 is really clear. Can we make the BPS vs. district average contrast more visible?
Love the color scheme. Red and black feels right for a contract campaign. Approved.
Can we make the June 17 date larger? It needs to be the first thing people see.
Done - moved the date to headline size. Also added BPS Central Office address at bottom.
HAPP stands for Historical context, Audience, Purpose, Point of view. We should add a key at the bottom of the organizer.
Newsletter masthead looks great. Can we add the chapter meeting date in the footer?
The photo of the march in Lawrence is excellent. Source credit should read: Library of Congress, 1912.
Instagram graphic size looks good. Should we also make a Twitter/X version at 1200x675?
The period boundaries are slightly off for Period 5. Should be 1844-1877, not 1850-1877.
Gettysburg trip flyer - please add the permission slip deadline: April 25, 2026. Bold it.
The comparison chart is persuasive but we need to double-check the Cambridge comparison data. I will send updated figures.
History Club logo looks great. Can we try a sepia or aged parchment variant for the secondary version?
Slide 4 needs a callout box for the grievance procedure steps. Should not be buried in bullets.
Period 8 slides - add the Domino Theory graphic we used last year. It really helps with the containment policy explanation.
BEU red hex is #C8102E not #CC0000 - please update the wear red day flyer.
Homepage wireframe - the Events section should appear above the Documents section. BEU members check events most.
Lawrence strike timeline looks great. Would love to use this with my English classes too if you share the file.
Contract rights one-pager - add a callout for the three-step grievance process. Members always ask about this.
Primary source annotation template - the HAPP acronym box should be collapsible for when students are ready to work without scaffolding.
April newsletter is ready to export. Header photo is approved, body text is proofread. Good to send.
